title: Workflow runtime naming and retention
summary: Pancreator workflow runtime directories MUST use sortable UTC names and harness-owned archival after seven days; agents MUST NOT rename or relocate generated run state manually.
instructions: Workflow directories under runtime/logs/workflows/ and the legacy mirrored runtime/workflows/ location MUST use <days-to-anchor>_<MMM-DD>-<minutes-to-end-of-UTC-day>_<eight-character-UUID>, with the minute field zero-padded to four digits.

DATETIME_ANCHOR MUST remain 2200-01-01T00:00:00.000Z. days-to-anchor MUST be the floor of the UTC duration from run creation to the anchor divided by one day, and minutes-to-end-of-UTC-day MUST be the ceiling of the remaining UTC-day duration in minutes.

The harness-owned pan archive command MUST migrate recognized legacy workflow directory names, update persisted path references, and move active-root workflow directories older than seven days into an archive/ child of the same parent directory.
Archived workflow directories MUST be excluded from active run discovery and repeated archival MUST be idempotent. Existing archive targets MUST never be overwritten silently.
Embedded installation refresh with install --yes MUST run the same naming migration and seven-day archival before replacing the owned harness payload, while preserving workflow contents.

Agents MUST NOT hand-rename, hand-move, or manually rewrite generated workflow directories or records. They MUST use pan archive or the installer-owned migration path for these deterministic operations.
